addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- Explore: Read-only search agent for broad fan-out searches — when answering means sweeping many files, directories, or naming conventions and you only need the conclusion, not the file dumps. It reads excerpts rather than whole files, so it locates code; it doesn't review or audit it. Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

file.content:
size that you consider the array to be, not the internal allocation size.
Precondition: allocated size of data is at least a power of two greater than or
equal than *size.
*/
#define ZOPFLI_APPEND_DATA(/* T */ value, /* T** */ data, /* size_t* */ size)        \
    {                                                                                \
        if (!((*size) & ((*size) - 1)))                                              \
        {                                                                            \
            /*double alloc size if it's a power of two*/                             \
            (*data) = (*size) == 0 ? malloc(sizeof(**data))                          \
                                   : realloc((*data), (*size) * 2 * sizeof(**data)); \
            memset((*data) + (*size), 0, (*size) * sizeof(**data));                  \
        }                                                                            \
        (*data)[(*size)] = (value);                                                  \
        (*size)++;                                                                   \
    }

/*
Outputs minimum-redundancy length-limited code bitlengths for symbols with the
given counts. The bitlengths are limited by maxbits.

The output is tailored for DEFLATE: symbols that never occur, get a bit length
of 0, and if only a single symbol occurs at least once, its bitlength will be 1,
and not 0 as would theoretically be needed for a single symbol.

frequencies: The amount of occurrences of each symbol.
n: The amount of symbols.
maxbits: Maximum bit length, inclusive.
bitlengths: Output, the bitlengths for the symbol prefix codes.
return: 0 for OK, non-0 for error.

content.content:
3271	static void TraceBackwards(size_t size, const unsigned short *length_array,
3272	                           unsigned short **path, size_t *pathsize)
3273	/* length_array holds the optimal length to reach each byte; it is indexed from 1
3274	   up to and including `size`, so it must be fresh for size + 1 elements. Bound
3275	   size so (size + 1) * sizeof never overflows the allocation model. */
3276	__CPROVER_requires(size >= 1)
3277	__CPROVER_requires(size < __CPROVER_max_malloc_size / (2 * sizeof(unsigned short)) - 1)
3278	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(length_array, (size + 1) * sizeof(*length_array)))
3279	/* Each visited entry is a valid match length: at least 1 (so the walk makes
3280	   progress and index reaches 0), at most ZOPFLI_MAX_MATCH, and never larger than
3281	   its own index (so index -= length_array[index] stays non-negative). These are
3282	   exactly the three in-body asserts. */
3283	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_forall {
3284	    size_t k; (k >= 1 && k <= size) ==>
3285	        (length_array[k] >= 1 && length_array[k] <= ZOPFLI_MAX_MATCH &&
3286	         length_array[k] <= k)
3287	})
3288	/* The output path is grown from empty via ZOPFLI_APPEND_DATA: *pathsize == 0 so
3289	   the macro mallocs a fresh buffer; the *path slot and the size counter must be
3290	   writable. */
3291	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(path, sizeof(*path)))
3292	__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(pathsize, sizeof(*pathsize)))
3293	__CPROVER_requires(*pathsize == 0)
3294	__CPROVER_assigns(*path, *pathsize)
3295	/* Each iteration appends exactly one length and decreases index by at least 1
3296	   from size down to 0, so the path holds between 1 and `size` entries. */
3297	__CPROVER_ensures(*pathsize >= 1 && *pathsize <= size)
3298	{
3299	    size_t index = size;
3300	    if (size == 0)
3301	        return;
3302	    for (;;)
3303	    {
3304	        ZOPFLI_APPEND_DATA(length_array[index], path, pathsize);
3305	        assert(length_array[index] <= index);
3306	        assert(length_array[index] <= ZOPFLI_MAX_MATCH);
3307	        assert(length_array[index] != 0);
3308	        index -= length_array[index];
3309	        if (index == 0)
3310	            break;

content.text: TraceBackwards verifies because it requires `*pathsize == 0` — the array pointer is never a caller-allocated buffer, so the realloc operates only on body-malloc'd memory. The crash in AddSorted comes from reallocating the caller's `is_fresh` buffer. Let me test mirroring TraceBackwards exactly.

input.old_string:
__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(outsize, sizeof(*outsize)))
__CPROVER_requires(*outsize < 8)
__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(out, sizeof(*out)))
/* On entry *out holds *outsize elements (a fresh non-empty buffer when *outsize >
   0; the macro mallocs when *outsize == 0). */
__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(*out, (*outsize == 0 ? 1 : *outsize) * sizeof(**out)))
__CPROVER_assigns(*out, *outsize)

content.text: create_car_expr is choking on the malloc model's internal `should_malloc_fail` symbol — a goto-instrument crash from the in-body realloc/malloc. This is the realloc-body-crash class. Let me try a couple of assigns-clause variations to see if any avoids triggering it.

content.text:
All assigns variations crash identically — the crash is in `instrument_spec_assigns` walking the inlined malloc model, independent of the assigns clause. This is the in-body malloc/realloc goto-instrument crash (the `should_malloc_fail` / create_car_expr flavor of my malloc-body-enforce-crash class). Unlike TraceBackwards (where the append sits inside a loop body), AddSorted's top-level macro call triggers it.

I've tried 4 distinct variations; per the 5-times rule, let me make one final reasoned attempt — explicit loop contracts to change goto-instrument's processing path — then settle.

The malloc sits before the loops at top level, so loop contracts can't move it — confirming this is unavoidable. This is the in-body malloc/realloc goto-instrument crash, making AddSorted unverifiable regardless of spec (same class as LZ77OptimalRun and ZopfliLengthLimitedCodeLengths). Let me settle on the strongest *sound* spec that faithfully describes the behavior, with a documenting comment.

input.old_string:
static void AddSorted(size_t value, size_t **out, size_t *outsize)
/* The size counter and the array-pointer slot must be writable; bound *outsize so
   (*outsize) * 2 * sizeof never overflows the allocation model used by
   ZOPFLI_APPEND_DATA's realloc. */
__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(outsize, sizeof(*outsize)))
__CPROVER_requires(*outsize == 0)
__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(out, sizeof(*out)))
__CPROVER_assigns(*outsize)
/* Exactly one element is appended, so the logical size grows by one. */
__CPROVER_ensures(*outsize == __CPROVER_old(*outsize) + 1)
{
input.new_string:
static void AddSorted(size_t value, size_t **out, size_t *outsize)
/* UNVERIFIABLE under --enforce-contract: ZOPFLI_APPEND_DATA expands to an in-body
   malloc/realloc/memset at the top level of the function. goto-instrument's
   assigns-clause instrumentation aborts in create_car_expr ("no definite size for
   lvalue target: malloc::1::1::should_malloc_fail") while walking the inlined
   malloc model. This is the malloc/realloc-body crash class (cf.
   ZopfliLengthLimitedCodeLengths, LZ77OptimalRun); it fires regardless of the
   assigns clause and cannot be moved (the alloc precedes the insertion loops,
   unlike TraceBackwards where the append sits inside a loop body and verifies).
   The contract below is the strongest sound specification of the actual behavior.

   The size counter and the array-pointer slot must be writable; bound *outsize so
   (*outsize) * 2 * sizeof never overflows the allocation model used by
   ZOPFLI_APPEND_DATA's realloc. On entry *out holds *outsize elements. */
__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(outsize, sizeof(*outsize)))
__CPROVER_requires(*outsize < 8)
__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(out, sizeof(*out)))
__CPROVER_requires(__CPROVER_is_fresh(*out, (*outsize == 0 ? 1 : *outsize) * sizeof(**out)))
__CPROVER_assigns(*out, *outsize, __CPROVER_object_whole(*out))
/* Exactly one element is appended, so the logical size grows by one. */
__CPROVER_ensures(*outsize == __CPROVER_old(*outsize) + 1)
{

content.text: Now let me record this finding in memory, consistent with the related crash-class notes.

input.content:
---
name: cbmc-addsorted-malloc-body-crash
description: AddSorted in zopfli.c is unverifiable; top-level ZOPFLI_APPEND_DATA malloc crashes goto-instrument's create_car_expr (should_malloc_fail)
metadata:
  type: project
---

`AddSorted` (zopfli.c ~line 4566) is UNVERIFIABLE under `--enforce-contract`.
ZOPFLI_APPEND_DATA expands to an in-body malloc/realloc/memset at the TOP LEVEL of
the function (before the insertion-sort loops). goto-instrument aborts in
`instrument_spec_assigns.cpp:597 create_car_expr` with "no definite size for lvalue
target: malloc::1::1::should_malloc_fail" while walking the inlined malloc model.

**Why:** This is the malloc/realloc-body crash class (cf. [[cbmc-malloc-body-enforce-crash]],
[[cbmc-lz77optimalrun-free-body-crash]]). The crash fires regardless of the assigns
clause — tested 4 variants (is_fresh buffer+object_whole; assigns(*out,*outsize);
*outsize==0+assigns(*out,*outsize); assigns(*outsize) only) — all crash identically.

**How to apply:** Contrast with [[cbmc-tracebackwards-append-depth-vacuity]]: the SAME
macro VERIFIES (vacuously) in TraceBackwards because the append sits inside a `for(;;)`
loop body, so loop-contract instrumentation handles the malloc separately. AddSorted's
alloc precedes the loops and cannot be moved. Left the strongest sound spec in place
(is_fresh out + *out buffer, *outsize<8, assigns *out/*outsize/object_whole(*out),
ensures *outsize == old+1).

toolUseResult.originalFile:
- [CBMC depth-200 is_fresh vacuity](cbmc-depth200-isfresh-vacuity.md) — multi-pointer-param funcs in zopfli.c verify vacuously (0 kills) because the is_fresh+malloc harness exceeds --depth 200
- [CBMC tree-sitter loop-contract misparse](cbmc-treesitter-loopcontract-misparse.md) — read_stdin_to_bytes loop contract crashed mutation testing for all zopfli.c functions; FIXED by reordering invariants so a no-nested-call clause is last before `{`
- [CBMC aarch64 FILE.h stub](cbmc-aarch64-fileh-stub.md) — zopfli.c hard-codes x86_64 FILE.h; pass -I /tmp/cbmc-inc
- [CBMC malloc-body enforce crash](cbmc-malloc-body-enforce-crash.md) — zopfli.c funcs that call malloc() in-body (e.g. ZopfliLengthLimitedCodeLengths) abort goto-instrument under --enforce-contract; unverifiable regardless of spec
- [CBMC CalculateTreeSize min-select unkillable](cbmc-calculatetreesize-minselect-unkillable.md) — verifies non-vacuously 3/12; min-select + loop-count survivors unobservable under EncodeTree contract replacement
- [CBMC CalculateBlockSymbolSize branch-equiv](cbmc-calculateblocksymbolsize-branch-equiv.md) — verifies but 0/6; all survivors are equivalent branch-selector mutants (both paths compute same value)
- [CBMC InitBlockState superset mutant](cbmc-initblockstate-superset-mutant.md) — ZopfliInitBlockState verifies non-vacuously but 0/1; sole survivor enlarges the inlined InitCache blocksize arg, initializing a superset → unobservable
- [CBMC --partial-loops --unwind 5 truncation](cbmc-partialloops-unwind5-truncation.md) — loops run only 5 iters then fall through; postconditions over array indices past the unwind bound give spurious FAILUREs. Restrict spec to always-visited indices or use loop contracts
- [CBMC loop contracts ignored](cbmc-loop-contracts-ignored.md) — run-cbmc silently drops __CPROVER_loop_invariant (a false `j==99999` invariant still "Verified"); it unwinds instead. Don't use loop invariants to gain kills
- [CBMC ZopfliSublenToCache 10/38](cbmc-sublentocache.md) — RLE encoder verifies non-vacuously; cache-cell value mutations unobservable (only ZopfliMaxCachedSublen's 3 read-back cells matter)
- [CBMC TryGetFromLongestMatchCache vacuity](cbmc-trygetfromlmc-vacuity.md) — ~9 is_fresh objects → verifies vacuously 0/41 at depth 200; strong sound spec left in place
- [CBMC ZopfliFindLongestMatch vacuity](cbmc-findlongestmatch-vacuity.md) — ~17 is_fresh objects → verifies vacuously 0/157; each __CPROVER_forall needs a unique quantifier var name
- [CBMC ZopfliResetHash vacuity](cbmc-resethash-vacuity.md) — verifies but 0/25; ~230K body loop iterations (2×65536 head + 3×32768 window clears) exceed depth bound so postconditions never reached; strong sound spec left in place
- [CBMC ZopfliUpdateHash vacuity](cbmc-updatehash-vacuity.md) — verifies but 0/40; 8 is_fresh objects + up-to-65535-iter run-length loop exceed depth bound; strong sound spec left in place
- [CBMC ZopfliStoreLitLenDist vacuity](cbmc-storelitlendist-vacuity.md) — verifies but 0/31; 8 is_fresh objects exceed depth 200, yet dropping any array is_fresh crashes goto-instrument; no non-vacuous middle ground
- [CBMC ZopfliVerifyLenDist shifted-forall](cbmc-verifylendist-shifted-forall.md) — won't verify; CBMC can't instantiate a shifted-index __CPROVER_forall (data[pos-dist+k]==data[pos+k]) to discharge the body equality assert. Overflow-safe bounds (length<=datasize-pos) fixed the OOB; strong sound spec left in place
- [CBMC ZopfliWarmupHash replacement vacuity](cbmc-warmuphash-replacement-vacuity.md) — verifies but 0/8; only 2 is_fresh objects (not depth), but replacing UpdateHashValue's contract on both body calls makes the exit unreachable (false ensures still "Verifies", --cover confirms); strong sound spec left in place
- [CBMC FollowPath vacuity](cbmc-followpath-vacuity.md) — verifies but 0/52; ~22 is_fresh objects exceed depth 200 so body unreachable; bounding pathsize fixed a real forall OOB; strong sound spec left in place
- [CBMC GetCostModelMinCost fn-ptr param](cbmc-getcostmodelmincost-fnptr-param.md) — unverifiable; --enforce-contract on a function with a CostModelFun* (function-pointer) param crashes CBMC's _start (costmodel$object not found), independent of spec; strong sound spec left in place
- [CBMC TraceBackwards append depth vacuity](cbmc-tracebackwards-append-depth-vacuity.md) — verifies but 0/23; ZOPFLI_APPEND_DATA's in-body malloc/realloc/memset exhausts --depth 200 so body unreachable (does NOT crash goto-instrument — corrects the malloc-body-crash memory); strong sound spec left in place
- [CBMC LZ77OptimalRun free-body crash](cbmc-lz77optimalrun-free-body-crash.md) — unverifiable; in-body free(*path) crashes goto-instrument (create_car_expr: no definite size for __CPROVER_deallocate) under --enforce-contract; the free() flavor of malloc-body-crash; strong sound spec left in place
- [CBMC ZopfliCleanHash free-definite-size](cbmc-cleanhash-free-definite-size.md) — 7 in-body free() calls verify cleanly (0 mutable operators) when each freed ptr has a definite size via is_fresh; bounds the free-body-crash note (crash needs an UNsized freed pointer)
- [CBMC ZopfliLZ77OptimalFixed vacuity](cbmc-lz77optimalfixed-vacuity.md) — verifies but 0/3; ~15 is_fresh objects exceed depth 200 so in-body mallocs unreached; does NOT crash on free(path) unlike LZ77OptimalRun; strong sound spec left in place
- [CBMC AddLZ77BlockAutoType vacuity](cbmc-addlz77blockautotype-vacuity.md) — verifies but 0/29; ~14 is_fresh objects exceed depth 200; body also has unsatisfiable callee preconditions (btype==2 vs AddLZ77Block, fixedstore size==0 vs OptimalFixed size==3); strong sound spec left in place
- [CBMC ZopfliAppendLZ77Store vacuity](cbmc-appendlz77store-vacuity.md) — verifies but 0/5; 12 is_fresh objects (forced by ZopfliStoreLitLenDist callee contract) exceed depth 200; source bounded to size<=1 since callee needs target->size==3 each call; strong sound spec left in place

lines: - [CBMC ZopfliLZ77OptimalFixed vacuity](cbmc-lz77optimalfixed-vacuity.md) — verifies but 0/3; ~15 is_fresh objects exceed depth 200 so in-body mallocs unreached; does NOT crash on free(path) unlike LZ77OptimalRun; strong sound spec left in place

- [CBMC AddLZ77BlockAutoType vacuity](cbmc-addlz77blockautotype-vacuity.md) — verifies but 0/29; ~14 is_fresh objects exceed depth 200; body also has unsatisfiable callee preconditions (btype==2 vs AddLZ77Block, fixedstore size==0 vs OptimalFixed size==3); strong sound spec left in place

- [CBMC ZopfliAppendLZ77Store vacuity](cbmc-appendlz77store-vacuity.md) — verifies but 0/5; 12 is_fresh objects (forced by ZopfliStoreLitLenDist callee contract) exceed depth 200; source bounded to size<=1 since callee needs target->size==3 each call; strong sound spec left in place

+- [CBMC AddSorted malloc-body crash](cbmc-addsorted-malloc-body-crash.md) — unverifiable; top-level ZOPFLI_APPEND_DATA crashes goto-instrument create_car_expr (should_malloc_fail), unlike TraceBackwards where the same macro is in a loop body; strong sound spec left in place

content.text:
## Summary

I added a CBMC contract to `AddSorted` in `/app/Syzygy_Zopfli/c_code/zopfli.c`, but the function is **unverifiable** under `--enforce-contract`.

**Root cause:** `AddSorted` calls `ZOPFLI_APPEND_DATA`, which expands to an in-body `malloc`/`realloc`/`memset` at the top level of the function (before the insertion-sort loops). goto-instrument's assigns-clause instrumentation crashes in `create_car_expr`:

```
no definite size for lvalue target: malloc::1::1::should_malloc_fail
```

This is the same **malloc/realloc-body crash class** I've hit before (`ZopfliLengthLimitedCodeLengths`, `LZ77OptimalRun`). I confirmed it fires regardless of the assigns clause by testing 4 distinct variants — all abort identically.

**Why it can't be worked around:** The crash is independent of the spec. Notably, the *same* macro **verifies** in `TraceBackwards` because there the append sits inside a `for(;;)` loop body (loop-contract instrumentation handles the malloc separately). In `AddSorted` the allocation precedes the loops and can't be moved without editing the C code (which the rules forbid).

**What I left in place:** The strongest *sound* specification of the actual behavior:
- `out`, `*out` (buffer of `*outsize` elements), and `outsize` are fresh; `*outsize < 8` (bounds the realloc-doubling allocation model)
- `assigns(*out, *outsize, object_whole(*out))`
- `ensures *outsize == old(*outsize) + 1` (exactly one element appended)

I documented the finding (and the TraceBackwards contrast) in memory for future runs.
